A cohesive visual identity for a contemporary art gallery, built around the tension between raw materials and refined presentation.
Primary logo application on archival card stock
Color palette exploration — warm neutrals with saturated accent
Brand system in context — environmental signage mockup

A cohesive visual identity for a contemporary art gallery, built around the tension between raw materials and refined presentation. The system uses a single heavy typeface against generous negative space, letting the work breathe.

A visual inquiry into the graphic language of China's Cultural Revolution — propaganda posters, iconographic hierarchies, and the aesthetics of mass mobilization.
Cover — banner composition, red and black ink on newsprint
Red Guard — geometric uniformity and collective identity
Four Olds — pictographic erasure and the aesthetics of revision
Black Five — the grammar of political classification
Representative — the hero and the masses

A visual inquiry into the graphic language of China's Cultural Revolution. Each poster is analyzed as a complete visual system: type hierarchy, color politics, symbolic compression, and the spatial grammar of mobilization. The question driving the work: what does it mean for a design language to be weaponized?

Layout design for a long-form essay on architecture and memory, published in the Winter issue of Folio Magazine.
Opening spread — full bleed photography with overlaid title
Body text grids showing column rhythm and footnote placement
Closing spread with pull quote and colophon
Alternate cover concept — matte laminated finish

Layout design for a long-form essay on architecture and memory, published in the Winter issue of Folio Magazine. The challenge was holding the reader's attention across twelve pages without a single image beyond the opening and closing spreads.

A series of twelve editorial illustrations for a feature on urban ecology, each drawing the reader into a different scale of city life.
Scale study: the sidewalk crack as landscape
Process spread — thumbnailing and compositional studies
A series of twelve editorial illustrations for a feature on urban ecology, each drawing the reader into a different scale of city life — from the microbial to the architectural.

A motion system for a music festival — dynamic identities for twelve stage posters, animated to feel like a single living organism.
Main stage title card — grid dissolve transition
Secondary stage variation — warm color temperature shift
Motion keyframes showing easing and overshoot on type

A motion system for a music festival — dynamic identities for twelve stage posters, animated to feel like a single living organism. Each poster has its own color temperature, but the timing and rhythm are shared.

A series of oil paintings exploring domestic objects under natural light.
Study in glass and ceramic — morning north light
Cloth and porcelain — the weight of linen
Final composition — vessels in afternoon window

A series of oil paintings exploring domestic objects under natural light. Each composition draws from the quiet intimacy of everyday items: ceramic vessels, glass, cloth. Arranged and rearranged until the light tells you where to look.

The work began as a study in translucency and evolved into an inquiry into the domestic still life as a site of attention. What does it mean to slow down and look?

A typographic poster series exploring the relationship between letterform and texture — each piece treats type as material rather than as vector.
Poster one — heavy ink on uncoated stock, overprinted twice
Poster two — letterform dissection, halftone separation
Poster three — ink wash and pressure variation on cotton rag
Poster four — registration test print with bleed marks

A typographic poster series exploring the relationship between letterform and texture — each piece treats type as material rather than as vector. The constraint: one typeface, four inks, no digital effects.
